content.text:
Ghostty's vim mode is called **scroll mode** (or "keyboard scroll mode"). Here's how it works:

## Enter scroll mode
`Ctrl+Shift+Space` — enters scroll/selection mode

## Navigation (vim keys)
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `h/j/k/l` | move cursor left/down/up/right |
| `w/b` | word forward/backward |
| `0` / `$` | start/end of line |
| `g` / `G` | top/bottom of scrollback |
| `Ctrl+u` / `Ctrl+d` | half page up/down |

## Selection & copy
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `v` | start visual selection |
| `V` | visual line selection |
| `y` | yank (copy) selection → clipboard |
| `Enter` | also copies and exits |

## Exit
`Esc` or `q` — exit scroll mode

---

Your config doesn't override any of these, so defaults apply. The trigger is `Ctrl+Shift+Space`.
